description:
You are helping a junior script-developer who needs a tiny utility for quickly extracting the most severe log entries out of an existing log file.

1. A directory named “/home/user/data” already exists and contains a single file called “server.log”.  
2. Create an executable Bash script called “extract_cf.sh” in “/home/user/data”.  
   • The script must accept no parameters and must always read “/home/user/data/server.log”.  
   • It shall output (to **stdout**) only the lines whose text contains the exact tokens “[CRITICAL]” **or** “[FATAL]”.  
   • It must preserve the original order and print the lines verbatim.  
3. After creating the script, run it exactly once and redirect its output to a new file named “/home/user/data/critical_fatal.log”.  
4. Produce a small statistics file “/home/user/data/summary.txt” with this exact, two-line format (no extra spaces, no additional lines):  

CRITICAL=<number_of_lines_containing_[CRITICAL]>  
FATAL=<number_of_lines_containing_[FATAL]>  

For example, if four lines matched and two of them contained “[CRITICAL]”, the file must read:  
CRITICAL=2  
FATAL=2  

The automated grader will check all of the following:  
• “/home/user/data/extract_cf.sh” exists, is executable, and contains some form of a grep/awk/sed or similar regex filter.  
• Running “/home/user/data/extract_cf.sh” prints exactly the expected four lines when executed in the container.  
• “/home/user/data/critical_fatal.log” exists and contains the same four lines in the same order.  
• “/home/user/data/summary.txt” exists and matches the format above with the correct counts.  
Avoid hard-coding the counts inside the script; the grader will re-run the script during evaluation.  

Do not reveal the log contents in your answer. You only need to write the script, run it once, and create the summary file.
truth:
# Pre-existing file before the agent starts
Path: /home/user/data/server.log  
Contents (exactly 10 lines, trailing newline after each):  
2024-04-21 14:03:22 [INFO] Starting server  
2024-04-21 14:03:23 [WARN] Low disk space  
2024-04-21 14:03:25 [CRITICAL] Database connection failed  
2024-04-21 14:03:26 [INFO] Retrying connection  
2024-04-21 14:03:28 [CRITICAL] Database connection failed  
2024-04-21 14:04:01 [ERROR] Could not write to log  
2024-04-21 14:05:12 [FATAL] Kernel panic  
2024-04-21 14:06:30 [INFO] Shutdown initiated  
2024-04-21 14:06:35 [FATAL] Unable to recover  
2024-04-21 14:06:40 [INFO] Shutdown complete  

Lines that must be extracted (in this order):  
2024-04-21 14:03:25 [CRITICAL] Database connection failed  
2024-04-21 14:03:28 [CRITICAL] Database connection failed  
2024-04-21 14:05:12 [FATAL] Kernel panic  
2024-04-21 14:06:35 [FATAL] Unable to recover  

Expected counts:  
CRITICAL lines = 2  
FATAL lines     = 2  

Files that must exist after task completion:

1. /home/user/data/extract_cf.sh  
   • Any shebang (#!/usr/bin/env bash recommended).  
   • Must use a regex-capable tool (grep -E, awk, sed, etc.) to search for “\[CRITICAL]” or “\[FATAL]”.  
   • Exit code 0 on success.  

2. /home/user/data/critical_fatal.log  
   • Must contain only the four lines listed above, each followed by a newline, in the same order.  

3. /home/user/data/summary.txt  
   • Two lines, exactly:  
     CRITICAL=2  
     FATAL=2  
   • A final newline after the second line is acceptable but not mandatory.
name: task_000000_d2271c33
